name: google-home-blade-mcp
title: Google Home
description: Google Home / Nest device control via SDM API — thermostats, cameras, doorbells, event streams
tagline: Nest thermostat, camera, and doorbell control via SDM
author: groupthink-dev

highlights.text: Thermostats — read temperature, set mode, adjust setpoints, fan control
Cameras — stream URLs, snapshot events, motion and person detection
Doorbells — press events, chime status, visitor detection
Event streams — real-time Pub/Sub for device state changes
Structure-aware — devices grouped by rooms and structures
Write-gated — reads always work, commands require explicit opt-in

readme:
### What it does

Google Home Blade connects your AI agent to Google Nest devices through the Smart Device Management (SDM) API. It provides tools for reading device state, controlling thermostats, viewing camera streams, monitoring doorbell events, and subscribing to real-time device event streams via Pub/Sub.

Your agent can check the current temperature across your home, adjust thermostat setpoints, view camera feeds, and react to doorbell presses or motion events — all through a structured tool interface.

### Device support

The SDM API exposes Nest thermostats, cameras, and doorbells. Devices are organised by structure (home) and room, so your agent understands the physical layout. Supported device traits include temperature, humidity, HVAC mode, fan control, camera live stream, and event history.

### Safety model

All tools follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** work immediately — list devices, check temperature, view camera status
- **Commands** require `GOOGLE_HOME_WRITE_ENABLED=true` — set thermostat, change mode, toggle fan

Event streams (Pub/Sub) are read-only and available without the write gate.

### Setup complexity

This plugin has the most involved setup of any Blade MCP — it requires a Google Cloud project, SDM API access ($5 USD one-time Device Access Console fee), OAuth 2.0 credentials, and optionally a Pub/Sub subscription for event streams. The `make auth` command handles the OAuth flow once credentials are configured.

env.name: GOOGLE_HOME_CLIENT_ID
env.description: OAuth2 client ID from Google Cloud Console

env.name: GOOGLE_HOME_PROJECT_ID
env.description: Project ID from Device Access Console

setup.help_text: Requires a Google Cloud project with SDM API enabled, Device Access Console registration ($5 USD one-time), and OAuth2 credentials. Run 'make auth' to complete the OAuth flow and obtain the refresh token.

fields.label: Refresh Token
fields.help: Run 'make auth' in the repo to complete the OAuth flow and obtain this token

prerequisites: Google Cloud project with Smart Device Management API enabled
Device Access Console registration ($5 USD one-time fee)
OAuth 2.0 credentials (Web application type) with redirect URI configured

scenarios.label: Evening comfort routine
scenarios.body: Check current thermostat readings across the house, lower the temperature in bedrooms for sleeping, and confirm the changes took effect.

scenarios.label: Review doorbell activity
scenarios.body: Pull recent doorbell press events and camera motion detections, summarise visitor activity for the day, and check if any events occurred during away hours.

scenarios.label: Monitor home climate while away
scenarios.body: Subscribe to thermostat state changes via Pub/Sub, alert if temperature drifts outside a safe range, and adjust HVAC mode remotely if needed.
